findings.evidence: grep shows only forward lemmas at `FundamentalGroup.lean:74`, `:81`, `:91`, while the generic fibre equivalence in `Topology/Homotopy/Covering.lean:79` does provide an inverse characterization; roadmap `Targets.lean:15` names the deck-to-π₁ direction.
findings.fix: Add a symmetric characteristic lemma, e.g. for `g : (Deck p)ᵐᵒᵖ`, `(hp.monodromy ((hreg.fundamentalGroupEquiv hp e).symm g) e : E) = g.unop • (e : E)`, and preferably the `Deck p` spelling with `MulOpposite.op φ`; mark it `[simp]` if it is the intended normal form.
findings.issue: `IsRegular.fundamentalGroupEquiv` exposes a `FundamentalGroup X x ≃* (Deck p)ᵐᵒᵖ`, but the API only states how a loop maps forward; there is no public lemma characterizing `(hreg.fundamentalGroupEquiv hp e).symm` on a deck transformation, so the stated downstream direction `Deck(proj) ≃* π₁(X,x₀)` cannot be used without deriving it ad hoc from `eq_iff`.

summary: The forward comparison is characterized, but the exposed API does not characterize the inverse direction needed by the roadmap’s deck-to-fundamental-group target.
